title: Those huge mirror building things
wikitext:
[]: Those huge mirror building things             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Oct 31, 2015 3:23 pm I can't remember, but do they say what they even do in the anime/manga? If not, I was recently playing through GOS2 and noticed this snippet of info.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 31, 2015 3:32 pm I can't remember either, but it's one of those sufficiently obvious things from what we see to infer that there are light-pipes down into the GeoFront. The phrase "light-pipe" hasn't been used before on the forums, so I suspect that this is the first time that this has been discussed (certainly the first time I've been in such a thread and said anything about the mechanism).

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Sat Oct 31, 2015 3:33 pm They do not say AFAIK, I always assumed it was a source of solar power. Although that is a neat little factoid.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Oct 31, 2015 3:42 pm I thought there was some kind of mirror relay system built into the roof of the cavern to project light downwards. If you push it too far, the construction of the Geofront makes no sense. There's now way you could build something with that internal volume with no supports, even a dome, and where are the launch tubes for the Evangelions? If they exit all over the city they should stick up through the Geofront all over the place.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Oct 31, 2015 4:06 pm The TV series never makes it clear but in 2.0 they make pretty clear those things are giant solar panels. They probably spin around in whatever direction the sun is coming from & it would make sense they also reflect sunlight down into the Geofront. Pretty smart design for solar panels actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Oct 31, 2015 4:57 pm There is also one in the geofront as well, right behind Nerv. I agree that they're probably solar panels.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Oct 31, 2015 5:52 pm In 2.0's slice of life montage, they turn to catch light from the rising sun!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 01, 2015 4:54 pm I noticed years ago that they were labeled "light condensing buildings" in the settei, and the rest fell into place readily. Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: There is also one in the geofront as well, right behind Nerv.

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Sun Nov 01, 2015 5:58 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Pretty smart design for solar panels actually.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Nov 02, 2015 12:12 am Chuckman wrote: If you push it too far, the construction of the Geofront makes no sense. There's now way you could build something with that internal volume with no supports, even a dome, and where are the launch tubes for the Evangelions? If they exit all over the city they should stick up through the Geofront all over the place.

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Mon Nov 02, 2015 12:53 am It's really just a fps arena setup they don't have to think about. Eva spawns, pick up spawns (guns), starting weapons (a knife and some kind of shoulder needles), map markers for direction (the nerv pyramid), wrapped up in sea and mountains as borders. The cables make the navigation inefficient but the real game starts with the 5 minutes countdown like in any online round ever. Actually a complaint on architecture you can make is that all of the buildings are too near to each other without a single thought to either human or eva space, nor things like the launch tubes or more map markers, with everything looking samey but yeah again it's not something they had to think about. It really gets better in eoe and its combat when they drop the whole thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 02, 2015 12:55 am I always assumed they went to the wall and then up. We know those launch elevators make some crazy turns, so it all fits. It's dumb , but it fits.

[]: Fun for the the whole cynical fandumb! BP             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Nov 02, 2015 1:19 am Ah, found it (finally): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PtdHyPMLYKY#t=12m27s Figured they were just for solar power until I saw this. In hindsight it explains how the viewer perceived "sunset" the first time we saw the Geofront in episode 1. These videos, they're awkwardly translated, but frickin' hilarious.
